cards.back: MERN is an acronym for MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js, a set of popular technologies used for full-stack development.
Serverless refers to a cloud computing model where the cloud provider manages the infrastructure, and the user only pays for the compute time used.

MERN components refer to the individual technologies that make up the MERN stack: MongoDB for data storage, Express.js for server-side logic, React.js for the user interface, and Node.js for server-side execution.

cards.front: What is the purpose of React.js in MERN?
cards.back: React.js is a JavaScript library used for building user interfaces, and it is used in the MERN stack to create reusable UI components.

cards.front: What is Node.js used for in MERN?
cards.back: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime environment that allows developers to run JavaScript on the server-side, and it is used in the MERN stack to execute server-side logic and interact with databases.
